attributes.description: Alerts when a Lambda function exceeds a configurable throttle rate (Throttles / (Invocations + Throttles)) over a 15-minute window.
attributes.name: [AWS Lambda OTel] High throttle rate

investigation_guide.blob:
## High Throttle Rate

### Triage Steps
1. Compare throttle rate with `ConcurrentExecutions` peak to confirm capacity saturation.
2. Review whether traffic increased or function duration grew, holding concurrency longer.
3. Check reserved vs unreserved concurrency allocation for the function.
4. Evaluate whether provisioned concurrency or a concurrency limit increase is needed.

### Note
This rule uses throttle rate rather than raw count, which is useful for high-traffic functions where a small absolute throttle count may still represent a significant rejection rate.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-aws.lambda.otel-*
| WHERE Namespace == "AWS/Lambda"
    AND stat == "Sum"
    AND MetricName IN ("Throttles", "Invocations")
| STATS
    total_throttles = SUM(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/Lambda/Throttles`),
    total_invocations = SUM(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/Lambda/Invocations`)
  BY FunctionName, cloud.region
| EVAL denominator = total_invocations + total_throttles
// Minimum traffic to avoid noisy alerts on idle functions
| WHERE denominator > 10
| EVAL throttle_rate_pct = ROUND(total_throttles / denominator * 100.0, 2)
// Alert threshold (%): lower toward 0 for strict no-throttle policies
| WHERE throttle_rate_pct > 0.5
| EVAL function_id = CONCAT(FunctionName, "/", cloud.region)
| SORT throttle_rate_pct DESC
| LIMIT 10
